channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 96% of Ransomware Victims Are Small Businesses — How to Actually Protect Your Stack
item.description: Verizon's newly-released 2026 DBIR found that 96% of ransomware victims (where organization size was known) were small and medium-sized businesses. Third-party involvement in SMB breaches has...

item.title: After 2.5 years of nights and weekends, I finally shipped my Angular view builder
item.description: I started building this on January 21st, 2024. After work, weekends, holidays whatever free time I could find. Two and a half years later, I finally decided to ship it as a beta. The idea is...

item.title: pretty interesting stuff!!
item.description: I built a CLI so I'd stop typing ImageMagick flags wrong. shekhar shekhar shekhar Follow Jul 22 I built a CLI so I'd stop typing ImageMagick flags wrong. #cli #javascript #opensource #ai 10...

item.title: Resend Prometheus Monitoring: Alert on Email Bounces in Grafana
item.description: If you are looking for Resend Prometheus monitoring, Resend Grafana alerts, or a way to alert on email bounces, that is exactly what resend-exporter is for. It is a small Prometheus exporter and...

item.title: No Human at the Keyboard: OpenAI's Models Escaped Their Sandbox and Hacked Hugging Face to Cheat a Benchmark
item.description: TL;DR what: OpenAI disclosed that GPT-5.6 Sol and an even more capable pre-release model escaped a highly isolated evaluation sandbox and attacked Hugging Face's production infrastructure to cheat...

item.title: The smartest engineers I know are afraid to decide
item.description: Forty-five minutes. That's how long a brilliant engineer I've worked with spent presenting a comparison matrix for a backend framework. Performance benchmarks, learning curve, documentation...

item.title: I reshaped a mature marketplace platform into a free "give it away" app - Laravel + Inertia + Vue + PostGIS
item.description: Hey everyone, Quick backstory on why I built this. Giving away stuff you no longer need through Facebook groups or social media is a mess: those platforms were never built for it. It's a race to...

item.title: One Support Call, Four Bugs: A Postmortem on Our AI Website Builder
item.description: I discovered a page on our customer's site that, at first glance, appeared to be a CSS bug. A hero section on her site had collapsed into a vertical sliver — a fashion-photo strip about 70 pixels...

item.title: 7 Tuya Matter Pitfalls That Will Kill Your Certification (And How We Avoided Them)
item.description: # 7 Tuya Matter Pitfalls That Will Kill Your Certification (And How We Avoided Them) We have shipped several Tuya Matter products over the past eighteen months. Every single project hit at least...

item.title: I Built a Free Privacy-First Developer Toolkit - No Data Leaves Your Browser
item.description: As developers, we use online tools every day. JSON formatters JWT decoders Base64 encoders Hash generators URL encoders Diff tools And many more. But there's one question we don't always ask:...

item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
item.description: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

item.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
item.description: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia, with commitments to responsible energy, community investment, jobs, and access to Codex.

item.title: 3 Google updates from Galaxy Unpacked 2026
item.description: Gentle Monster glasses, Warby Parker glasses, a prompt asking for the history behind a pictured building, and a prompt asking to book a table at a pictured restaurant

item.title: Samsung’s newest foldable finally feels Ultra
item.description: While we wait for Apple's rumored foldable iPhone, Samsung is polishing a design it already has on the market. I just spent three hours with my hands on the new Z Fold 8 Ultra, and all I can think...

item.title: Samsung’s wider Z Fold 8 feels just right
item.description: A year after overhauling its Z Fold phone with a radically thinner design, Samsung has changed things up again, this time altering the shape entirely. The Z Fold 8 is wider than any of Samsung's...

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 bet big on battery
item.description: It's a year of refinement for the Galaxy Watch. With the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, which are being announced today, Samsung is more or less taking what people like about its...

item.title: I almost forgot Samsung’s Z Flip 8 was a foldable
item.description: Samsung's new Galaxy Z Flip 8 feels more like a regular phone than ever. It's thinner, lighter, and has a front screen you can do just about anything on. Whether that's a good or bad thing, I'm...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 2026: The 6 biggest announcements
item.description: Samsung's annual summer event kicked off on Wednesday morning with the company unveiling a new lineup of watches and foldables. Following weeks of rumors and leaks, Samsung announced the Galaxy Z...

item.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
item.description: A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS...

item.title: Samsung Unpacked 2026: all the news from the July foldable launch
item.description: Samsung revealed its newest slate of foldables at this summer’s Galaxy Unpacked, which took place in London. In addition to a Galaxy Z Fold 8 that adopts a wider, passport-like form factor,...

item.title: The Download: NASA’s new space telescope and OpenAI’s autonomous hacker
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could...

item.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
item.description: Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need,...

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted...

item.title: Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections
item.description: The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing...

item.title: First-Person Identity Theft Story
item.description: Harrowing story of an identity theft victim. Yes, the person made a mistake—they gave the scammer a two-factor authentication code that allowed the scammer to take over their email address. But...

item.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
item.description: IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitments and employ its own tools" to help quell the spread of deceptive generative AI content across platforms. Meta...

item.title: The 2026 Honda Prelude is a marvel of hybrid technology
item.description: When it comes to enthusiast-geared Honda hardware, the Civic Si, Civic Type R, and Acura NSX often come to mind first for their revvy VTEC (Honda's form of variable valve timing) engines and...

item.title: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
item.description: In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about...

item.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
item.description: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.
